checked_paths: all audited dependency repos searched for the listed symbols
negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: No local audited repo contains ExecutorRegistered or ExecutorUpdated log decoding from log.data.as_ref(), nor any payload-length reporting path for those event payload bytes.
related_but_insufficient_code: ritual-spc-verification/src/types.rs still defines ExecutorRegistry and deactivate_executor, but it is only an in-memory map and has no log-watching service.
ritual-tee-registry-reader reads TEEServiceRegistry storage via TEE_SERVICE_REGISTRY_CONTRACT_ADDRESS; it does not decode ExecutorRegistry event logs.

ritual-reth-nodebuilder-internal/crates/node/src/executor.rs syncs registry state from storage before execution but uses TEE_SERVICE_REGISTRY_CONTRACT_ADDRESS and read_all_executors, not ExecutorRegistry event receipts.

notes: Confirmed by exact event-symbol searches across all audited local repos; the current local architecture reads registry storage instead of consuming ExecutorRegistry event logs.
rationale: The primary commit message says Alloy `Log` stores data directly as `Bytes`, and the diff changes both event decoders from `log.data.data` to `log.data.as_ref()`. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: SPC verification must decode `ExecutorRegistered` and `ExecutorUpdated` event payloads from `log.data.as_ref()` and use the same log data bytes when reporting payload length.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create Alloy logs for `ExecutorRegistered` and `ExecutorUpdated` with valid ABI-encoded data, pass them to the SPC service decoding helpers, and assert decoding succeeds and extracts the expected fields; mutate the log data bytes and assert the helper returns `InvalidEventData`.
summary: Executor event payload decoding from log data bytes is absent.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create Alloy logs with ABI data for ExecutorRegistered/Updated; no local handler exists to decode log.data.as_ref() or report payload length.
title: Executor events decode from log data bytes
